headline: RM28B Data Centre Boom Strains Selangor's Water and Power
context: Selangor's data centre corridor attracted RM28 billion in investments by 2025, but the 42 approved facilities require 850 MW of power and 120 million litres of cooling water daily — straining infrastructure originally built for residential use.

cards.big: 42 data centres consuming 850 MW — enough electricity for 2 million homes — while Selangor faces water rationing
cards.sub: A single hyperscale data centre uses more water than a town of 50,000 people for server cooling

cards.big: Data centres pay industrial electricity rates of 42 sen/kWh but receive 10-year tax holidays worth RM4.2 billion collectively
cards.sub: Net fiscal contribution questionable when tax incentives are deducted from the relatively modest 8,200 direct jobs created

cards.big: Cyberjaya and Sepang residents report 12-hour water rationing during peak data centre cooling demand periods
cards.sub: Residential communities compete for the same water infrastructure originally designed for 40% of current demand levels

cards.big: MIDA approved 42 data centres without cumulative infrastructure impact assessment — each evaluated individually
cards.sub: No single agency tracks aggregate resource demand from data centres — approvals happen in isolation from infrastructure planning

cards.big: Approving 42 data centres without checking if there's enough water and power is like selling 42 seats on a 20-seat bus
Should data centre approvals require mandatory infrastructure sufficiency certification before construction permits are issued?
